titleStmt.title: The Acting Secretary of State to the Secretary of State, at Paris

head.hi: The Acting Secretary of State to the Secretary of State, at Paris

div.p:
Gadel 476. Urtels Delgas 739 and 740Both dated November 13; neither printed, but see footnotes 1 and 2, supra. were sent last night to President for instructions which have not yet been received. Meanwhile
I have talked with Clifford by telephone and we agree that: 1.
Language in second pghThis paragraph read: “Decides that, in order to eliminate this threat to the peace and to facilitate the transition from the present truce, without prejudice to its application under the resolution of 4 November, to permanent peace in Palestine, an armistice shall he immediately established in all sectors of Palestine”. (501.BB Palestine/11–1348) Delga 740 with reference to SC resolution of November 4 is equivocal. 2.
As a matter of practice within realm of possibility, sanctions applied by SC in instant case are out of question. 3.
We can not place ourselves in position of “being against peace”.

Accordingly, on constructive side, we feel that utmost emphasis should be placed on getting both parties to war in Palestine to negotiate armistice and eventual peace. If you make it clear that draft resolution contained in Delga 746 Supra. is but a step to this paramount end, I feel that, under authorization in Gadel 420 November 7Not printed; but see footnote 1 to telegram Delga 634, p. 1556. you are free to vote for that draft resolution. (This telegram is commentary on Gadel 475Dated November 14, not printed, but see footnote 2, p. 1583. which precedes.)
Attitude toward Soviet amendments to Bundle’s draft resolution (Delga 634Dated November 6, p. 1555.) has not yet been cleared with President and if these amendments come to vote you should abstain.
